A video showing National Guard personnel stationed around the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool in Washington, D.C., sparked widespread criticism and ridicule on social media. The deployment comes amid ongoing controversy surrounding the Reflecting Pool after renovation work ordered by the Trump administration reportedly resulted in algae growth and other maintenance problems. Footage licensed by FreedomNews.tv showed uniformed Guard members standing near the pool while visitors walked through the area and cleanup equipment remained nearby.
Several public figures and commentators questioned the purpose of the security presence. Former Republican National Committee chair Michael Steele sarcastically asked whether the troops were there to protect the pool from algae. Other critics mocked the situation by describing the deployment as a major security operation focused on safeguarding a decorative pond. Some commentators also criticized the potential use of taxpayer resources for guarding

item.title: Anthony Scaramucci Highlights Trump's Past Remarks About Discrediting the Press
item.description:
Former White House communications director Anthony Scaramucci renewed criticism of President Donald Trump by drawing attention to a past exchange between Trump and journalist Lesley Stahl of CBS's '60 Minutes.' According to Scaramucci, Stahl once asked Trump why he frequently labeled media reports as 'fake news.' Scaramucci recalled that Trump responded by saying that he needed to discredit journalists so that people would not believe negative reporting about him. Scaramucci argued that this comment provides insight into what he sees as a long-term political and communications strategy employed by Trump.
In a social media post, Scaramucci described the remark as a candid admission of intent rather than an isolated comment. He contended that Trump has consistently sought to undermine the credibility of critics, journalists, and institutions in order to reduce the impact of unfavorable coverage. Scaramucci further claimed that Trump has often succeeded by applying relentless pressure an

item.title: Caviar unveils luxury magnetic rear panel accessories for iPhone 17 Pro Max featuring exotic materials
item.description:
Luxury customization brand Caviar has introduced a new line of premium magnetic rear panel accessories designed for the iPhone 17 Pro Max. Rather than replacing the entire device, these add-ons attach via Apple’s MagSafe system and can be removed when not in use. The collection, called Relict Magnetic Custom, is aimed at high-end buyers seeking unusual materials and exclusivity in smartphone design.
The most extravagant version features a titanium-backed panel embedded with elements such as alligator leather and a fragment of what is marketed as a T-Rex tooth. This top-tier model is extremely limited, with only seven units reportedly available, and carries a price of approximately R74,000 (around $4,500). According to the report, the company positions it as a collector-style luxury accessory rather than a mass-market product.
Other variations in the lineup are slightly more accessible but still highly expensive. The Proteus edition, for example, uses titanium combined with blue croco

item.title: Adam Kinzinger Claims Reflecting Pool Damage Was Caused by Maintenance Chemicals Rather Than Sabotage
item.description: A dispute has emerged over the cause of damage to the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool after former Republican Congressman Adam Kinzinger challenged claims that the deterioration was the result of political sabotage. According to Kinzinger, the damage was likely caused by maintenance efforts carried out by personnel associated with the Trump administration rather than by outside actors. In a series of social media posts and a video, he argued that concentrated hydrogen peroxide was added to the pool to eliminate algae growth that appeared shortly after a costly renovation project. Kinzinger contends that the chemical treatment stripped or weakened the paint coating inside the basin, leading to the peeling and visible damage now attracting public attention. He cited information indicating that industrial-strength hydrogen peroxide can act as a powerful oxidizing agent capable of degrading paint binders and causing surfaces to blister or peel. His explanation directly contradicts stateme

item.title: Rare Behind-the-Scenes Photos Highlight Nicole Kidman’s Breakthrough Performance in Dead Calm (1989)
item.description:
The article showcases 26 behind-the-scenes photographs of Nicole Kidman during the production of the 1989 psychological thriller 'Dead Calm,' a film widely regarded as a turning point in her career. At just 21 years old, Kidman played Rae Ingram, a woman struggling with grief after the death of her young son. Seeking recovery, Rae and her husband John, portrayed by Sam Neill, embark on a sailing trip in the Pacific Ocean. Their journey takes a dangerous turn when they rescue Hughie Warriner, played by Billy Zane, a disturbed survivor from a sinking vessel. As events unfold, Rae finds herself trapped aboard a yacht with a dangerous psychopath, leading to a tense battle for survival.
The article emphasizes how Kidman’s performance distinguished her from many of her contemporaries. Rae evolves from an emotionally devastated woman into a resourceful and determined survivor who relies on intelligence, resilience, and courage rather than brute force. Kidman reportedly learned sailing and ya

item.title: Larry Sabato Says Republican Prospects Depend on Improving Trump's Standing With Independent Voters
item.description:
Political analyst Larry Sabato, director of the University of Virginia Center for Politics and editor of the Crystal Ball newsletter, argued during an MSNBC appearance that Donald Trump continues to dominate the Republican Party and remains its most influential figure. Sabato suggested that the GOP has effectively become the 'Trump party,' noting that Trump's endorsements still carry significant weight in Republican primary elections, even if they do not always guarantee victory.
However, Sabato identified a major challenge for Republicans heading into the 2026 midterm elections. While the MAGA movement remains highly motivated, he estimated that its core support represents only about 35 percent of the electorate. According to Sabato, winning general elections requires broader appeal, particularly among independent voters. He pointed to polling showing that Trump receives strongly negative ratings from independents, with some surveys indicating unfavorable or poor job-approval numbers

item.title: Trump Claims Additional Arrests and Expanded Damage Allegations in Reflecting Pool Investigation
item.description:
President Donald Trump stated in a Truth Social post that additional people have been arrested in connection with alleged vandalism at the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool in Washington, D.C. According to Trump, the damage was more extensive than previously reported. He claimed that individuals used a knife or blade to create a 250-foot-long gash in the pool’s facade and poured corrosive chemicals into the water. Trump described the alleged acts as an insult to American history and specifically referenced Presidents George Washington and Abraham Lincoln.
The president said he had met with contractors to discuss repairs and indicated that a significant portion of the pool may need to be drained to complete restoration work. He also praised the recent condition of the pool, asserting that it had been functioning perfectly and looked better than at any time since its original construction in 1922.
However, the article notes that Trump did not provide evidence to support the new allegati

item.title: Book Reports Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent Privately Compared Trump to George Soros and Criticized Zelenskyy
item.description:
A forthcoming book titled 'Regime Change,' written by New York Times reporters Maggie Haberman and Jonathan Swan, reportedly contains several revelations about Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent's private views regarding President Donald Trump and Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy. According to excerpts described in the article, Bessent told associates that Trump reminded him of billionaire investor and Democratic donor George Soros, for whom Bessent previously worked during his Wall Street career. The book quotes Bessent as saying that Trump and Soros were 'the same animal,' a comparison considered notable because Soros has long been a frequent target of criticism within conservative political circles. The authors characterize this as a private assessment made by a senior Trump administration official.
The book also describes Bessent's alleged comments about Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy before a contentious Oval Office meeting in February 2025. According to the reporte

item.title: A Retrospective of Irving Penn’s Influential Vogue Cover Photography During the 1950s
item.description:
This article highlights the remarkable Vogue magazine covers created by renowned photographer Irving Penn throughout the 1950s. Widely regarded as one of the most influential photographers of the twentieth century, Penn developed a distinctive visual style characterized by minimalist compositions, controlled studio lighting, and a strong emphasis on the subject rather than elaborate backgrounds or props. His approach helped redefine fashion photography and elevated it to the status of fine art.
The feature presents a chronological collection of Vogue covers photographed by Penn between 1950 and 1959. Many of the era’s most celebrated models appeared in these images, including Jean Patchett, Lisa Fonssagrives-Penn, Bettina Graziani, Suzy Parker, Anne St. Marie, Joanna McCormick, Monique Chevalier, and a young Jane Fonda. The covers demonstrate Penn’s ability to capture elegance, sophistication, and individuality through simple yet powerful portraiture.
According to the article, Penn’s

item.title: Report on voting machines finds no evidence of fraud amid claims of delayed release by Trump administration
item.description:
A former Republican congressman, Adam Kinzinger, has criticized the Trump administration over its handling of a government-commissioned report examining allegations of voting machine fraud. According to Kinzinger, the investigation was initiated following an executive order from President Donald Trump aimed at investigating claims of “stolen elections,” particularly focusing on whether voting machines could alter or flip votes. The effort was intended to substantiate long-standing allegations of widespread election fraud.
However, reporting attributed to Reuters indicates that the Office of the Director of National Intelligence (ODNI) produced findings showing no evidence that voting machines used or planned for use in upcoming midterm elections were capable of switching votes or otherwise manipulating election outcomes. Kinzinger stated that the report concluded “not a single vote was switched anywhere,” directly undermining claims of systemic machine-based fraud.
Despite these find

item.title: Lavrov Criticizes European Ukraine Policy and Calls for a New Eurasian Security Framework
item.description: The article reproduces a text attributed to Russian Foreign Minister Sergei Lavrov that was reportedly intended for publication in Politico Europe but was not ultimately published. In the article, Lavrov argues that European countries are not neutral participants in the Ukraine conflict but active supporters of Ukraine's effort to defeat Russia. He claims that Western institutions, including NATO and the European Union, have spent decades expanding eastward and undermining Russian security interests. The text reviews events from the 2004 Orange Revolution, the 2014 change of government in Ukraine, the Minsk agreements, and the breakdown of security negotiations before Russia's 2022 invasion. Lavrov accuses European governments of encouraging anti-Russian policies and using diplomacy to conceal geopolitical expansion. He rejects recent proposals from the United Kingdom, France, Germany, and Ukraine for a negotiated settlement, describing them as an ultimatum rather than a basis for peac

item.title: Critics dispute Trump's vandalism claim over Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool renovation problems
item.description:
A controversy emerged after President Donald Trump claimed that vandalism was responsible for problems affecting the recently renovated Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool in Washington, D.C. Trump posted on Truth Social that law enforcement was investigating alleged acts of vandalism after the pool's water turned green and sections of newly applied blue paint began peeling. He suggested that political opponents were responsible and linked the incident to markings that had recently appeared on the National Mall. However, he did not provide evidence identifying suspects or proving sabotage.
The claim drew criticism from several public figures and commentators. Former Republican Congressman Adam Kinzinger mocked the explanation and argued that Trump was refusing to acknowledge failure. Other critics, including writer Amanda Carpenter and former CIA officer Patrick Skinner, suggested that the problems were the result of poor management rather than deliberate damage.
According to the articl

item.title: Congressman Criticizes DOJ Decision to End Environmental Investigation Into Senator Jim Justice's Coal Operations
item.description: Rep. Mike Levin (D-California) publicly criticized the Trump administration after reports emerged that the U.S. Department of Justice ended a federal criminal investigation into coal companies owned by Sen. Jim Justice (R-West Virginia) and managed largely by his son, Jay Justice. According to reporting cited by Levin, the investigation involved the Environmental Protection Agency, the DOJ's Environmental Crimes Section, and the U.S. Attorney's Office for the Western District of Virginia. Authorities had been examining whether repeated pollution violations committed by Justice-affiliated mining operations constituted criminal violations of the Clean Water Act. The companies were reported to have accumulated tens of thousands of alleged environmental violations over the past decade. Prosecutors reportedly believed they had a strong case and had initially received approval to proceed. However, officials in the Office of the Deputy Attorney General allegedly ordered the investigation to s

item.title: Critics react to Trump’s proposal to rename ICE as “NICE”
item.description:
Former President Donald Trump sparked online backlash after proposing a symbolic name change for U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE). In a Truth Social post, Trump suggested renaming the agency the “National Immigration and Customs Enforcement,” abbreviated as “NICE.” He claimed the change would confuse or “discombobulate” journalists and critics, framing it as a rhetorical tactic against what he called dishonest media coverage. The proposal quickly circulated on social media, where commentators and political analysts ridiculed both the idea and its intended effect.
Critics argued that the renaming effort appeared superficial and politically motivated rather than substantive policy reform. Several public figures and commentators mocked the acronym switch, pointing out that “ICE” and “NICE” sound nearly identical when spoken aloud, undermining any supposed attempt at confusion. Others framed the suggestion as an example of political messaging focused more on branding and onl

item.title: Trump Suggests Possible U.S. Fees for Shipping Through the Strait of Hormuz if Ceasefire Agreement Fails
item.description:
President Donald Trump proposed the possibility of imposing U.S.-controlled fees on ships traveling through the Strait of Hormuz if a recently established ceasefire arrangement in the region were to collapse. In a post on Truth Social, Trump stated that there would be no tolls in the strategically important waterway for a 60-day ceasefire period and that no tolls would continue afterward, except for charges that could be imposed by the United States. He argued that such fees would serve as compensation for what he described as America's role in protecting Middle Eastern countries and ensuring maritime security in the region.
The proposal follows heightened tensions involving Iran and a ceasefire that Trump claimed to have helped secure. According to the article, Trump framed potential future tolls as reimbursement for past, present, and future security-related costs borne by the United States. The remarks suggest a vision in which Washington could seek financial compensation for maint

item.title: Former Olympic Canoe Athlete Arrested After Incident at Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool
item.description: David Hearn, a three-time Olympic canoe slalom athlete, was arrested near the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool in Washington, D.C., after allegedly touching a section of paint that had already begun peeling from the recently renovated pool. According to reports, Hearn visited the site after a bicycle ride to observe the condition of the pool following a $14 million renovation project associated with the Trump administration. The Reflecting Pool has recently faced problems including algae blooms that turned the water green and deterioration of paint after workers used hydrogen peroxide in an effort to address the algae issue. Hearn stated that he merely reached into the water to feel a loose piece of paint that was already partially detached and denied causing any damage. Nevertheless, U.S. Park Police arrested him on a misdemeanor charge of destruction of government property. Video of the arrest, shared on social media by conservative journalist Emily Miller, showed several officers ha

item.title: Legal Commentators Question Gorsuch's Historical Reasoning in Supreme Court Gun and Marijuana Case
item.description:
The article discusses reactions from legal commentators Michael Popok and Lisa Graves to a unanimous U.S. Supreme Court decision in United States v. Hemani. The case involved a Texas gun owner who admitted to regularly using marijuana and was prosecuted under a federal law that restricts firearm possession by unlawful drug users. The Supreme Court ruled 9-0 that the government could not automatically disarm the individual based solely on the circumstances presented in the case, concluding that the prosecution violated protections under the Second Amendment.
During an episode of the Legal AF podcast, Popok and Graves focused their criticism on the opinion written by Justice Neil Gorsuch. They argued that part of Gorsuch's reasoning relied heavily on historical discussions about alcohol consumption in early American history. According to the commentators, the opinion examined how common drunkenness was among earlier generations and whether historical regulations on intoxicated individua

item.title: Anne Murray’s Stage Presence and Performance Style During the 1970s
item.description: The article looks back at Canadian singer Anne Murray’s live performances during the 1970s, a decade in which she became an international music star through hits such as “Snowbird,” “Danny’s Song,” and “You Needed Me.” Murray, who sold more than 55 million albums throughout her career and won four Grammy Awards, developed a stage presence that contrasted sharply with many of the glamorous pop stars of the era. Rather than relying on elaborate costumes or theatrical performances, she embraced a simple and approachable image that reflected her Nova Scotia roots and former career as a physical education teacher. Her typical stage wardrobe included bell-bottom jeans, casual shirts, pantsuits, and sneakers, complemented by her recognizable short hairstyle. The article emphasizes that Murray’s performances were centered on her vocal abilities rather than visual spectacle. Audiences and critics frequently praised her rich contralto voice and her ability to reproduce the same quality and emoti

item.title: Japan Approves Significant Increase in Foreign Visa Fees Effective July 2026
item.description: The Japanese government has approved a substantial increase in visa application fees for foreign nationals, marking the first revision of these charges since 1978. The decision was formally adopted during a cabinet meeting and will take effect for visa applications submitted on or after July 1, 2026. Under the new fee structure, the cost of obtaining a single-entry visa will rise from 3,000 yen to 15,000 yen, representing a fivefold increase. Similarly, the fee for a multiple-entry visa will increase from 6,000 yen to 30,000 yen. According to Japanese officials, the adjustment reflects the impact of rising prices and higher administrative costs accumulated over several decades without any fee revisions. Foreign Minister Toshimitsu Motegi stated during a press conference that the government considers the increase necessary in light of inflation and changing economic conditions. He also indicated that authorities do not expect the higher visa costs to have an immediate or significant eff

item.title: Maruti Suzuki Ertiga Facelift Expected by 2027 With Design Updates, New Features, and Underbody CNG Option
item.description: MotorOctane reports that Maruti Suzuki is preparing a facelifted version of the Ertiga MPV, one of India's best-selling seven-seater family vehicles. Although the Ertiga remains popular among families and fleet operators due to its practicality, affordability, and spacious cabin, the model has received only limited updates in recent years. The upcoming facelift is expected to refresh the vehicle's appearance with revisions to the grille, bumpers, headlights, taillights, alloy wheels, and exterior color options. Inside the cabin, Maruti is reportedly planning more significant improvements, including a larger infotainment touchscreen, wireless phone charging, an electric sunroof, and potentially a 360-degree camera system. While these additions may not match the extensive feature lists offered by competitors such as the Kia Carens and Carens Clavis, they would modernize the Ertiga and improve its appeal. Another notable development under consideration is an underbody-mounted CNG tank. Th

item.title: Journalist Alisa Valdes-Rodriguez Says Family Citizenship Dispute Delayed Her Planned Departure from the United States
item.description:
Raw Story reported on statements made by journalist Alisa Valdes-Rodriguez, who is known for her reporting on Jeffrey Epstein and alleged intelligence-community connections. According to the article, Valdes-Rodriguez said she had previously planned to leave the United States after claiming that she had been targeted because of her reporting. She has alleged that these incidents caused permanent injuries and were connected to individuals unhappy with her investigations, although the article presents these claims as her assertions rather than independently verified facts.
In a statement published on Substack, Valdes-Rodriguez said her departure from the country had been delayed because of legal and administrative issues involving her father. She claimed that records related to her father's naturalized U.S. citizenship had been removed or altered, which she attributed to actions connected to the Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) initiative under the Trump administration. Her fat

item.title: JD Vance Faces Criticism After Claim That Strait of Hormuz Was Open Is Contradicted by Iran's Subsequent Closure
item.description:
Vice President JD Vance became the target of widespread criticism on social media after comments he made regarding negotiations with Iran appeared to be contradicted by subsequent events. During an appearance on Fox & Friends Weekend, Vance expressed confidence in the Trump administration’s position and the progress of talks involving Iran. Referring to adviser Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner, Vance said that discussions were going well and asserted that the United States held a strong negotiating advantage. He also stated that the Strait of Hormuz was open, presenting the situation as evidence of successful diplomacy.
However, according to the article, only a few hours after Vance's remarks, Iran moved to close the Strait of Hormuz, a strategically important shipping route for global energy markets. The development quickly prompted criticism from journalists, political commentators, academics, and political activists, many of whom argued that Vance's statements were either premature

item.title: DHS World Cup social media post sparks controversy and undermines Trump immigration narrative, analysis says
item.description:
An analysis published by journalist Alexander Willis reports that a recent social media post from the U.S. Department of Homeland Security (DHS) has created controversy during the early stages of the 2026 FIFA World Cup. The post, shared on the official DHS account on X, featured three non-white American soccer players along with slogans such as “Defend the Homeland,” “One Nation. One Homeland. One Team,” and “OUR SOIL.” Critics, including commentator Mehdi Hasan in an analysis cited by the article, argued that the messaging carried anti-immigrant or xenophobic undertones while simultaneously undermining the administration’s own narrative.
The controversy intensified because the players shown in the image—Chris Richards, Sergiño Dest, and Folarin Balogun—are American athletes whose backgrounds highlight the diversity of the U.S. national team. Balogun, in particular, was noted for scoring twice in a recent World Cup match against Paraguay, reinforcing his role as a key contributor to

item.title: Trump criticized after Truth Social poll on how to spell insult toward Democrats
item.description:
Former President Donald Trump drew widespread online criticism and ridicule after posting a poll on his Truth Social platform asking followers to choose between two spellings of a derogatory term aimed at Democrats: “Dumocrat” or “Dumbocrat.” In his post, Trump suggested that users simply replace the letter “e” with “u” and repeated his claim that “many people don’t know, or assume, that DUMB ends in ‘b.’” The post quickly circulated beyond his platform, prompting a wave of reactions from political commentators, journalists, and activists who questioned both the focus and tone of the message.
Critics argued that the poll distracted from more serious international and domestic issues being debated at the time, including geopolitical tensions involving Iran and global economic concerns. Some commentators mocked the spelling discussion itself, noting that the basic premise appeared unnecessary or self-evident. Others used the moment to raise broader concerns about presidential conduct an

item.title: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent Reportedly Compared Donald Trump and George Soros in New Book
item.description:
A report highlighted by The Guardian and drawn from the book 'Regime Change' reveals that U.S. Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent privately compared President Donald Trump to billionaire investor and Democratic donor George Soros. According to the book, Bessent told associates that Trump reminded him of Soros, for whom he previously worked as chief investment officer at Soros Fund Management. Bessent reportedly described the two influential figures by saying, 'They are the same animal,' suggesting similarities in their personalities, leadership styles, or approaches to decision-making.
The comparison is notable because Soros has long been a frequent target of criticism from Trump and many supporters within the MAGA movement. Trump has publicly attacked Soros on multiple occasions and has even suggested investigating him. Bessent, however, has a professional history with Soros and is widely credited with helping manage investments that contributed to the billionaire’s wealth.
The book a

item.title: Book Claims Melania Trump Opposed White House Renovation Plans but Failed to Stop Major Changes
item.description: A discussion on The Daily Beast Podcast highlighted claims from the book 'Regime Change,' written by journalists Maggie Haberman and Jonathan Swan, regarding tensions between First Lady Melania Trump and President Donald Trump over significant alterations to the White House grounds and buildings. According to the commentators, the book describes Melania Trump as being unhappy with several renovation projects that proceeded despite objections from her team. One of the most notable disputes involved changes to the Rose Garden. The book reportedly states that President Trump initially wanted to remove not only portions of the garden but also the rose bushes themselves. Melania Trump was allegedly able to secure limited concessions, including preserving the roses, but could not prevent broader changes to the area. The discussion also referenced the demolition of the East Wing to make way for a new ballroom project. Commentators said that members of Trump's team understood that the decision

item.title: Trump Adviser Alex Bruesewitz Accuses Mark Levin of Undermining Administration’s Iran Peace Deal
item.description:
A public dispute erupted within pro-Trump circles after Trump adviser Alex Bruesewitz sharply criticized Fox News host Mark Levin over Levin’s opposition to a tentative peace agreement between the Trump administration and Iran. Levin, who had previously supported President Trump during the conflict with Iran despite widespread criticism of the war, changed course after details of the proposed agreement emerged. He argued that the administration had effectively surrendered to Iranian demands and continued to voice objections on social media.
The latest confrontation began when Levin mocked a social media post from President Trump that appeared to suggest he could influence Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu’s electoral prospects. Levin sarcastically questioned whether such behavior constituted interference in another country’s election.
In response, Bruesewitz posted a lengthy criticism on X, accusing Levin of repeatedly attacking Trump and referencing a report from The Times o

item.title: Italian Conservative Newspaper Criticizes Trump After Dispute With Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni
item.description:
A conservative Italian newspaper, Libero Quotidiano, published a strongly worded opinion article criticizing U.S. President Donald Trump following a public disagreement involving Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni. The controversy began after Trump reportedly stated during an interview that Meloni had 'begged' him for a photograph during the G7 summit in France. Meloni firmly rejected the claim, describing it as false and accusing Trump of inventing the story.
In response, Libero Quotidiano published an editorial that used the Italian word 'coglione,' a vulgar insult roughly equivalent to calling someone an idiot or fool. The article argued that Trump's remarks had unnecessarily damaged what many Italians viewed as a positive political relationship between the American president and Meloni. According to the editorial, only someone acting without intelligence would undermine diplomatic goodwill by making what it described as an obviously untrue statement.
The newspaper suggested th

item.title: DOJ Declines to Submit Court-Ordered Declaration on Suspended Anti-Weaponization Fund
item.description:
A dispute has emerged between the U.S. Department of Justice (DOJ) and a federal judge over the future of a proposed $1.776 billion anti-weaponization fund. According to the report, U.S. District Judge Leonie Brinkema previously issued an order indefinitely blocking the fund and directed the DOJ to submit a declaration under penalty of perjury confirming that the initiative would not move forward. The judge reportedly requested signatures from Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche, Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent, and Associate Attorney General Stanley Woodward, who were described as key officials involved in establishing the fund.
Instead of providing the requested declaration, DOJ attorney Andrew Block filed a different document on June 19. In that filing, the department argued that such a declaration was unnecessary and raised concerns that requiring testimony or sworn statements from senior executive branch officials could create separation-of-powers issues. The filing also pointe

item.title: Opinion column argues converging political and economic pressures could lead to Trump resignation
item.description:
The article is an opinion column that argues, in speculative terms, that a combination of political, economic, and internal party pressures could potentially lead to President Donald Trump resigning. The author introduces the idea of a “rogue wave” formed by three converging forces that together could destabilize his presidency.
The first factor described is an economic downturn, which the author suggests could create widespread public dissatisfaction and political backlash. The second factor is Trump’s age and perceived physical and cognitive decline, which the column claims is becoming more visible and increasingly discussed in media and political circles. The third and most significant factor, according to the piece, is growing frustration within segments of the Republican Party in Congress, who the author claims are becoming more openly critical of Trump’s leadership, priorities, and political direction.
The article argues that while each of these issues has existed independently

item.title: Trump Polls Supporters on Preferred Democratic Nickname as Iran-U.S. Agreement Faces New Setback
item.description:
President Donald Trump used his Truth Social platform on Saturday to ask supporters for input on a nickname he should use for Democrats, presenting a poll that compared the terms 'Dumocrat' and 'Dumbocrat.' In the post, Trump described the question as a 'very important matter' and explained the reasoning behind each spelling variation. He argued that 'Dumocrat' preserved more of the original word 'Democrat,' while 'Dumbocrat' made the intended insult more explicit. The poll allowed users to vote on their preferred option, and early results showed a clear preference for 'Dumocrat,' receiving roughly two-thirds of the votes reported in the article.
The social media activity occurred against the backdrop of more significant international developments involving relations between the United States and Iran. According to the report, Iranian military officials announced that the Strait of Hormuz had been closed again after alleging a violation of a memorandum of understanding between Washing

item.title: Political commentator Keith Edwards says FBI agents visited his home after social media posts about the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool project
item.description:
Political commentator Keith Edwards stated that FBI agents visited his home after he posted messages on social media regarding the controversial restoration of the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool in Washington, D.C. The restoration project, reportedly costing $14 million and ordered as part of preparations for the 250th anniversary of U.S. independence, has drawn criticism after reports of algae growth and peeling blue paint shortly after completion.
Earlier on June 19, Edwards posted online that he would pay anyone willing to send him a piece of the paint that was reportedly peeling from the pool. He also referenced the number '86.47' in the offer, a number that has recently attracted political attention because of its mention in a Justice Department case involving former FBI Director James Comey. Some conservative commentators have interpreted the phrase as implying a threat against President Donald Trump.
Later that day, Edwards informed his followers that FBI agents had arrived

item.title: Rare 1968 Photo Session Captures Brian Wilson’s Playful and Eccentric Humor
item.description:
A feature published by Vintage Everyday revisits a memorable 1968 photo session starring Brian Wilson of The Beach Boys. The photographs were taken by renowned Hollywood and music photographer Earl Leaf and are preserved through the Michael Ochs Archives/Getty Images collection. Conducted at a Los Angeles restaurant, the session presented Wilson in an unconventional and humorous way during a significant period of his life and career.
By 1968, Wilson had largely stepped back from regular touring activities with The Beach Boys and was entering a phase often associated with increased creativity, eccentric behavior, and personal challenges following the abandoned Smile project. Rather than producing traditional promotional portraits, the photo shoot emphasized his playful personality and willingness to participate in absurd visual gags.
The images show Wilson pretending to eat or bite a variety of unusual and inedible objects, including a vinyl record, a shoe, and a pepper grinder. Other

item.title: Opinion writer argues Knicks should avoid a White House visit after NBA championship
item.description: In this opinion article, David Fagin reflects on the New York Knicks' 2026 NBA championship and the debate surrounding a potential White House visit following their title victory. The author celebrates the team's historic achievement, noting that it ended a 53-year championship drought and united New York fans around a memorable season. However, he argues that the team's next decision—whether to accept an invitation from President Donald Trump—could have lasting consequences for how the championship is remembered. Fagin contends that a White House appearance would transform a sports celebration into a political statement and could be interpreted by critics as normalizing or endorsing the current administration. He discusses the tradition of championship teams visiting the White House but notes that NBA champions have generally not participated in such visits during Trump's presidency. The article also criticizes Knicks owner James Dolan, portraying him as politically aligned with Trump

item.title: A Look Back at the Career and Elegance of French Actress Estella Blain
item.description:
The article pays tribute to Estella Blain (1930–2011), a French actress remembered for her refined beauty, gentle screen presence, and contributions to European cinema during the 1950s and 1960s. Blain built a successful acting career in an era when French and Italian films were gaining international recognition. Known for her soft facial features, expressive eyes, and understated performances, she frequently portrayed romantic, sensitive, and emotionally complex characters.
Among her notable film appearances were roles in 'The Gambler' (1958), 'The Cossacks' (1960), and 'The Black Tulip' (1964). While she did not achieve the worldwide fame of some of her contemporaries, Blain earned admiration from audiences and critics for her elegance and natural acting style. Her performances reflected a quiet sophistication that distinguished her from many other actresses of the period.
The article primarily serves as a photographic retrospective, presenting a collection of vintage portraits tha

item.title: Bhutan's Gross National Happiness Policy and Its Record on Ethnic Expulsions and Human Rights
item.description: Bhutan, a small Himalayan kingdom, has long been praised internationally for pioneering the concept of Gross National Happiness (GNH) as an alternative to GDP-focused economic growth. Introduced by King Jigme Singye Wangchuck in the late 1970s, GNH emphasizes well-being, compassion, and Buddhist values over pure economic metrics. The idea was enshrined in the country's constitution and successfully promoted at the United Nations, leading to the establishment of the International Day of Happiness. However, the article argues this image is largely a successful marketing strategy that masks serious human rights issues. While promoting non-violence and happiness, Bhutan forcibly expelled around 150,000 Lhotshampa people of Nepali origin in the late 20th century, stripping many of citizenship and displacing them to refugee camps in Nepal and later resettling them in countries like the United States and Australia. This ethnic cleansing affected roughly one-sixth of the population. Remaining

item.title: Former Federal Prosecutor Questions DOJ Conduct in Broadview Six Case After Release of Transcripts
item.description:
Newly released transcripts from the Broadview Six case have prompted criticism from former federal prosecutor Andrew Weissmann, who argued that several actions taken by prosecutors from President Donald Trump's Department of Justice appear inconsistent with accepted legal and ethical standards. During an interview with journalist Adam Klasfeld on the program 'All Rise News,' Weissmann highlighted what he described as serious concerns regarding the prosecutors' handling of grand jury proceedings.
According to Weissmann, one of the most troubling revelations was an apparent acknowledgment by prosecutors that they selected a particular grand jury because they trusted its members. He argued that such reasoning could be interpreted as 'grand jury shopping,' a practice that raises concerns about impartiality and fairness in criminal investigations. Weissmann stated that prosecutors are expected to remain neutral and should not seek out juries perceived as more favorable to their case.
He a

item.title: Kennedy Center Keeps Tarps in Place After Court-Ordered Removal of Trump's Name
item.description:
The John F. Kennedy Center for the Performing Arts remains at the center of controversy after a federal court ordered the removal of President Donald Trump's name from the building's exterior. The court ruled that Congress authorized only President John F. Kennedy's name to appear on the facility, requiring the removal of Trump's name that had previously been added. Days after the ruling, tarps and scaffolding continue to cover the area where the lettering was located, leading to public speculation about whether the removal has been fully completed.
According to the article, Kennedy Center operations chief Matt Floca submitted a sworn declaration stating that Trump's name had been removed. Photographs reportedly captured evidence that at least some of the letters were taken down. However, the Kennedy Center has declined to provide a detailed explanation for why the covered area remains hidden from public view. A spokesperson stated that the tarps and scaffolding would remain in place

item.title: Studio Portraits Document the Variety of Women's Hairstyles in the Late 1960s
item.description: This article from Vintage Everyday presents a collection of 35 vintage studio portraits highlighting women's hairstyles from the late 1960s, a period marked by significant cultural and fashion changes. During this transitional era, hairstyling evolved away from the heavily structured and voluminous looks that had characterized the early part of the decade. Instead, women increasingly embraced more natural, individualized, and expressive styles that reflected broader social transformations taking place at the time. The featured portraits showcase a wide range of popular hairstyles, including long straight hair associated with the hippie movement, feathered layers, soft curls, flowing locks, and lingering examples of the bouffant style that had dominated earlier years. The collection illustrates how hair became an important form of personal expression as younger generations challenged traditional beauty standards and embraced ideals connected to freedom, self-expression, and countercultu

item.title: New Jersey Council Member John Alite Charged with Loan Sharking, Extortion, and Threats
item.description:
John Alite, a Republican member of the Englishtown Borough Council in New Jersey, has been arrested and charged with multiple offenses related to an alleged illegal lending operation. According to charges announced by New Jersey Attorney General Jennifer Davenport, Alite allegedly issued loans that exceeded legal interest rate limits and later used threats of violence to collect money and property from borrowers. The charges against him include theft by extortion, corporate misconduct, usury, and terroristic threats. Prosecutors also allege that he used his company, Straightened-Out Entertainment, as part of the scheme.
Davenport stated that businesses are expected to operate within the law and that authorities will pursue those who engage in fraudulent or coercive conduct. The case has drawn significant attention because of Alite’s well-known criminal past. Before entering local politics, Alite was associated with the Gambino crime family and worked closely with notorious mob figures

item.title: Support for Trump Among Latino Business Owners Declines Amid Concerns Over Immigration Policies and Tariffs
item.description:
A report highlighted a significant decline in support for President Donald Trump among Latino business owners during his second term. According to polling cited in the article, approval among this business community has fallen from 69% to 39%, raising concerns for Republicans ahead of the 2026 midterm elections. Latino voters were one of the demographic groups that showed increased support for Trump during the 2024 election, with many attracted by promises of economic growth and business-friendly policies.
However, the report indicates that some Latino entrepreneurs have become dissatisfied with the administration's approach to immigration and trade. Business owners interviewed said that policies such as tariffs and stricter immigration measures have negatively affected their economic outlook and business operations. Javier Palomares, CEO of the U.S. Hispanic Business Council, stated that many members of the organization feel disappointed because the improvements they expected under T

item.title: Silk Road Café Dispute Over Dublin Castle Closure Set for Arbitration Hearing
item.description: The legal dispute involving Dublin's Silk Road Café and its landlord, the Chester Beatty Library, is expected to move toward arbitration after the landlord announced plans to seek court approval to refer the matter to that process. The conflict arose after concerns that the café would be forced to close during Ireland’s European Union Presidency because of heightened security measures at Dublin Castle. Earlier in the week, the café operator, Hannah’s Coffee Shop Limited, obtained an interim injunction preventing the landlord from interfering with the business or removing its equipment. Following the court order, staff regained access to the premises, preserved food supplies, and resumed limited operations. During a court hearing, lawyers for the café stated that the Office of Public Works (OPW) had clarified it did not object to the café remaining open, provided all employees completed the required Garda security accreditation. The OPW explained that security restrictions at Dublin Cas

item.title: Republican Strategist Warns Trump-Meloni Rift Could Weaken U.S. Alliances During Global Tensions
item.description:
A political dispute between U.S. President Donald Trump and Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni has drawn concern from Republican strategist Doug Heye, who warned that the fallout could have broader implications for international alliances. The disagreement intensified after Meloni publicly criticized Trump for claiming that she had 'begged' to take a photograph with him during the G7 summit. Meloni rejected the assertion and released a video expressing her frustration.
Speaking on CNN's 'OutFront,' Heye described the conflict as both embarrassing and poorly timed. He noted that Meloni had originally been viewed by many observers as an ideological ally of Trump because of her right-wing political positions, leading many to expect a strong relationship between the two leaders. However, Heye argued that the breakdown in relations reflects a pattern seen with other political figures who have worked closely with Trump.
According to Heye, maintaining strong relationships with allies is

item.title: Best British Beaches for a Summer Heatwave: Expert Recommendations Across the UK
item.description: As a heatwave brings warmer weather to the UK, sea temperatures can reach the high teens, making swimming without a wetsuit more appealing. Travel experts from the Daily Mail have compiled a guide to some of the best beaches across Britain, highlighting a variety of scenic spots suitable for different preferences. In Cleethorpes, Lincolnshire, visitors can enjoy four miles of custard-colored sands with a Victorian resort charm, complete with promenades and fish and chip shops. South Milton Sands in Devon, owned by the National Trust, offers a clean, dog-friendly beach next to a nature reserve with coastal walks and rock pools. Scarborough's South Sands provides both lively town vibes and quieter rock pool areas. Remote options include Twin Beaches on Gigha in the Inner Hebrides for pure escapism and Rye Harbour in East Sussex, a pebbly beach ideal for birdwatching. Southport Beach in Merseyside features vast sands at low tide and a historic pier, while Sandbanks in Dorset has beautiful

item.title: Actress from 'Weightlifting Fairy Kim Bok Joo' Draws Attention for Noticeably Different Look in New Photos
item.description: The article discusses South Korean actress Jo Hye Jung, known for her role as Jung Nan Hee in the popular K-drama “Weightlifting Fairy Kim Bok Joo,” who recently became a trending topic in Korean media following new Instagram photos. In the images, she participated in a fashion shoot for the luxury brand Fendi, wearing a plunging neckline top and fitted bootcut jeans. The photos highlighted a more mature and polished image compared to her earlier acting roles, drawing significant attention online. Korean media outlets and social media users reacted strongly, with several headlines describing her appearance as “unrecognizable” compared to her character in the hit series. Jo Hye Jung, who is also known as the daughter of actor Jo Jae Hyun, has continued to share aspects of her daily life through social media, and this recent post quickly spread across entertainment news platforms. Publications such as Sports Chosun, Tenasia, and Newsen commented on her transformation, emphasizing how muc

item.title: How Shai Kaplan’s Iyengar Yoga Practice Shaped Her Recovery Journey and Inspired a Hospital Rehabilitation Program
item.description:
This feature article recounts the story of Shai Kaplan, an Israeli woman who spent much of her youth battling recurring brain tumors, severe pain, and significant vision loss. After multiple surgeries failed to fully resolve her condition and facing an uncertain future, she left Israel and traveled around the world. During her travels in India, she was introduced to Iyengar Yoga, a highly structured form of yoga developed by B.K.S. Iyengar. What began as a trial experience evolved into a years-long period of intensive practice at Iyengar’s institute in Pune.
Under the guidance of B.K.S. Iyengar and his daughter Geeta, Kaplan followed individualized yoga sequences designed around her medical limitations. Over time, she reported improvements in her physical abilities, greater independence, reduced suffering, and a renewed sense of control over her life. The article emphasizes that Iyengar never promised a cure for her tumors, instead focusing on improving function and helping her work w

item.title: Nevada Governor Joe Lombardo’s Transgender Sports Ballot Measure Misses the 2026 Ballot
item.description:
Nevada Governor Joe Lombardo announced that his proposed ballot initiative concerning transgender athletes in school sports will not appear on the state's 2026 ballot. According to Lombardo, legal delays and uncertainty surrounding the case prevented the initiative process from being completed within the required timeframe. The measure had previously been ruled legally valid by the Nevada Supreme Court, but procedural delays ultimately stopped it from advancing to voters this election cycle.
The proposal sought to restrict participation in school sports based on a student's sex assigned at birth. It would have required organizations overseeing school athletics to classify sports participation into male, female, or coeducational categories. Lombardo argued that the measure was intended to provide a long-term solution for protecting female athletics.
With the initiative delayed, the governor indicated that he will first seek legislative action during Nevada's next legislative session.

item.title: Relativity Space Plans Private Mars Orbiter with NASA Scientific Instruments for 2028 Launch
item.description:
Relativity Space, a U.S. aerospace company best known for developing the Terran R launch vehicle scheduled for debut around 2027, has announced an unexpected plan to send a Mars orbiter in 2028. The company, which has recently stabilized financially following acquisition by former Google CEO Eric Schmidt, is positioning this mission as the first in a broader series of scientific spacecraft developed in collaboration with philanthropic organizations.
The planned Mars orbiter does not yet have an official name, but its scientific payload will be provided by NASA. The mission includes a radar instrument designed to measure the thickness and properties of subsurface ice deposits on Mars, as well as a suite of atmospheric instruments known as Aeolus, supplied by NASA’s Ames Research Center. Aeolus is intended to study Martian winds and their role in transporting dust, water vapor, carbon dioxide, and other atmospheric gases. It will combine a wide-field camera, a radiometer to measure surf

item.title: Senate Scrutiny Grows Over Todd Blanche's Refusal to Formally End Controversial Federal Fund
item.description:
Acting U.S. Attorney General Todd Blanche is facing increased political and legal pressure after declining to provide a federal court with a written commitment that the Trump administration's controversial 'Anti-Weaponization Fund' will be terminated. During a discussion on MSNBC's 'MS NOW,' analyst Scott MacFarlane argued that Blanche's handling of the issue could create difficulties as he seeks Senate confirmation to become attorney general on a permanent basis.
MacFarlane highlighted concerns from Senator Thom Tillis, a Republican member of the Senate Judiciary Committee, who reportedly opposes the continuation of the fund and has raised questions about its management. According to the analyst, Tillis is not viewed as a consistently loyal ally of former President Donald Trump and may be skeptical of Blanche's assurances regarding the program. The two are expected to meet to discuss the matter before upcoming confirmation-related proceedings.
The controversy also involves federal J

item.title: Rare Behind-the-Scenes Photos Reveal Kathleen Turner’s Breakthrough Experience Filming Body Heat (1981)
item.description: The article showcases 25 behind-the-scenes photographs of actress Kathleen Turner during the production of the 1981 neo-noir film Body Heat, the movie that launched her career as a major Hollywood star. Directed by Lawrence Kasdan in his directorial debut, the film starred Turner opposite William Hurt and became known for its intense atmosphere, suspenseful storytelling, and sensual tone. Although the story takes place during a sweltering Florida heatwave, production occurred during an unusually cold Florida winter, creating significant challenges for the cast and crew. Actors had to hide their breath in cold air by holding ice cubes in their mouths before filming, while artificial sweat was applied to maintain the illusion of extreme heat. Crew members often wore heavy coats while filming scenes that appeared hot and humid on screen. Turner later reflected on the demanding nature of the production, particularly the emotionally and physically intense scenes. She described some filming

item.title: Russia May Permanently Seal the Leaking Zvezda Transfer Compartment on the ISS to Reduce Risk
item.description:
The article examines a possible response by Roscosmos to persistent air leaks originating from the Transfer Chamber (PrK) of the Russian Zvezda module aboard the International Space Station (ISS). Earlier in June 2026, tensions arose when Russian cosmonauts planned to drill and cut into the aging module in an attempt to locate and repair the source of the leaks. NASA reportedly objected to the procedure because of concerns about potential risks to station safety, leading astronauts to temporarily prepare for a possible emergency return to Earth. The repair effort was ultimately abandoned.
According to reports cited in the article, Russia may instead choose to permanently isolate the leaking compartment by closing the hatch that connects the PrK to the rest of the station. The leaks are believed to be cracks within the internal transfer structure rather than breaches of the station’s outer hull, making isolation a practical way to reduce atmospheric loss. While this would not repair th

item.title: 1964 Experimental Portrait Session of Gena Rowlands Reimagining Classic Paintings by Leo Fuchs
item.description:
This article explores a creative and experimental 1964 photo session featuring actress Gena Rowlands and photographer Leo Fuchs. The project, known as the “Master Painters” series, aimed to reinterpret famous classical artworks through innovative photographic techniques. Instead of relying on digital editing—nonexistent at the time—Leo Fuchs achieved surreal, painterly effects entirely in-camera. One of the most notable techniques involved the use of multiple physical mirrors carefully arranged around Rowlands to fragment her reflection, creating a cubist-style visual effect inspired by Picasso.
The session included reinterpretations of several iconic works of art and artistic styles. Rowlands posed in ways that evoked Leonardo da Vinci’s Mona Lisa, Rembrandt-style portraits, works inspired by Thomas Gainsborough, Francisco Goya, Renoir, and Hans Memling, as well as a Picasso-inspired fragmented composition. Each setup was carefully staged to mimic the composition, mood, and aesthetic

item.title: White House responds to reporter criticism of Iran deal with personal attack
item.description:
A White House rapid-response account sparked controversy after issuing a personal insult toward journalist Batya Ungar-Sargon, who criticized President Donald Trump's preliminary agreement with Iran. Ungar-Sargon, a NewsNation weekend host and self-described “MAGA lefty,” publicly broke with the administration over what she described as a humiliating diplomatic deal. She argued that the agreement, combined with Vice President JD Vance’s remarks blaming Israel for regional instability, represented a significant setback for U.S. foreign policy and international credibility.
During a broadcast interview, Ungar-Sargon called the agreement an “utter humiliation of the United States,” claiming that even Iran recognized it as a strategic win. She also sharply criticized Vance, accusing him of promoting narratives that unfairly shifted responsibility for Middle East tensions onto Israel. Her comments quickly circulated online and drew a direct response from the White House’s Rapid Response 47

item.title: Report: White House Staff Monitored Trump’s Trash After He Discarded Valuable Silverware
item.description:
According to a report based on an upcoming book by New York Times journalists Maggie Haberman and Jonathan Swan, White House staff during President Donald Trump’s second term began monitoring his trash after discovering he had been discarding potentially valuable government property. The book, titled “Regime Change,” is expected to be released soon and includes accounts of internal concerns about how presidential property was being handled.
The report, as cited by the Daily Mail, claims that staff intervention was prompted after Trump was found to have occasionally thrown away sterling silver utensils belonging to the White House. Sterling silver is composed of 92.5% pure silver mixed with other metals for durability, and individual pieces can carry significant monetary value. In this case, some items reportedly discarded could be worth more than $30 each.
Following this discovery, staff allegedly began “monitoring” trash disposal to prevent further loss of valuable items. The situat

item.title: Report alleges DOJ has withheld Epstein email records that may reference Trump connections
item.description:
A recent report cited by CBS News and summarized in Raw Story claims that the U.S. Department of Justice (DOJ) may still be withholding a substantial number of documents related to convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein, despite official statements that all relevant files had been released. According to the reporting, as many as three million pages of material allegedly remain unpublished. Among the most significant claims is that investigators and analysts have identified multiple Epstein email accounts, including addresses such as 'jeevacation@gmail.com,' 'jeeproject@yahoo.com,' and 'littlestjeff@yahoo.com.' While some communications from Epstein’s accounts have been made public in the form of screenshots and partial records, others reportedly remain undisclosed or heavily redacted.
The article highlights concerns raised about the 'littlestjeff@yahoo.com' account in particular, suggesting that it may contain additional correspondence from the early 2000s, a period when Epstein was k

item.title: George Will argues Trump presidency is weakening after controversial Iran agreement
item.description:
A conservative columnist argues that President Donald Trump’s political influence and leadership are entering a period of decline following a controversial agreement to end U.S. military involvement in Iran. According to George Will, writing in an opinion column, the administration’s handling of the conflict reflects deeper flaws in Trump’s leadership style, including self-absorption, impulsiveness, and disregard for institutional norms. Will describes the Iran memorandum of understanding as poorly negotiated and politically damaging, suggesting it undermines U.S. allies such as Israel while granting concessions to Iran. He further argues that the agreement highlights a broader pattern of presidential decision-making he characterizes as erratic and driven more by personal priorities than national interest.
The column also frames Trump’s presidency as increasingly unstable, noting that political consequences from past actions—such as disputes over the 2020 election and efforts to resha

item.title: Report: Last-Minute Chaos Surrounds Signing of U.S.-Iran Agreement at Versailles Dinner
item.description:
According to a report by Agence France-Presse cited in the article, the signing of a major U.S.-Iran agreement was marked by unexpected logistical confusion and shifting plans. The agreement had been publicly announced days earlier, but the formal signing process remained unsettled and improvised. President Donald Trump reportedly decided to sign the deal during a candlelit dinner at the Palace of Versailles in France, a decision made spontaneously during a high-profile diplomatic setting.
However, when the signing moment arrived, the official document had not been printed. This led Secretary of State Marco Rubio to urgently search for a printer within the palace in order to produce a physical copy of the agreement. Once the document was prepared, Trump signed it using a thick black marker while dinner dishes were still on the table, underscoring the informal and unplanned nature of the event.
The article describes a series of shifting plans surrounding the agreement, including earli

item.title: Acting Intelligence Director Bill Pulte Reportedly Considers Large-Scale Staff Changes Amid Debate Over Election Security Assessments
item.description:
According to reports cited by Raw Story, Bill Pulte assumed the role of acting Director of National Intelligence without Senate confirmation and may serve in the position for up to 210 days under the Federal Vacancies Reform Act, a period that would extend beyond the 2026 midterm elections. CNN reported that Pulte arrived with a list of employees and was considering dismissing a significant number of intelligence officials. President Donald Trump has publicly stated that he expects Pulte to investigate election-related matters and has suggested that acting status gives him greater flexibility to make personnel decisions.
The article also references reporting from Reuters indicating that the White House has delayed the release of an Office of the Director of National Intelligence (ODNI) report concerning voting machine vulnerabilities. Some officials reportedly believe the report does not support Trump's longstanding claims that the 2020 presidential election was stolen. Reuters report

item.title: Analysis of JD Vance's New Book Suggests He Is Positioning Himself Beyond the Trump Era
item.description:
A Raw Story article highlights an analysis by author Colin Dickey, published in The New Republic, concerning Vice President JD Vance's new book, 'Communion.' According to Dickey, the book provides insight into how Vance may be preparing for a future political career that extends beyond President Donald Trump's leadership and the current MAGA movement. Dickey argues that the book appears to signal Vance's belief that MAGA, as it exists today, may not remain politically dominant after Trump leaves the scene.
The analysis describes 'Communion' as an unusual work because its arguments and assumptions seem disconnected from current political realities. Dickey specifically points to what he views as Vance's effort to present himself as more center-right and pragmatic than his public role within the Trump administration might suggest. He argues that this positioning creates tension between the policies associated with the administration and the more measured tone adopted in the book.
One ex

item.title: U.S. Stocks Rebound as Semiconductor Shares Lead Market Recovery Following Fed-Induced Sell-Off
item.description: U.S. stock markets finished higher on Thursday, recovering from losses triggered the previous day by concerns over the Federal Reserve's policy outlook. The S&P 500 gained 1.08% to close at 7,500.58, while the Nasdaq Composite advanced 1.91% to 26,517.93. The Dow Jones Industrial Average posted a more modest gain of 72 points, ending at 51,564.70. The rebound was led by semiconductor stocks after President Donald Trump announced that Intel would partner with Apple to design and manufacture chips in the United States. Intel surged more than 10%, while Nvidia and Micron Technology also posted strong gains. The broader semiconductor sector benefited significantly, with the iShares Semiconductor ETF rising more than 6%. Investors viewed the announcement as a positive sign for domestic chip production and future AI-related infrastructure development. Despite lingering concerns following the Federal Reserve's latest meeting, where policymakers signaled the possibility of higher interest rate

item.title: Amazon MGM Drops Release Plans for Luca Guadagnino’s OpenAI-Themed Film ‘Artificial’
item.description: Amazon MGM Studios has decided not to release 'Artificial,' an upcoming film directed by Luca Guadagnino that focuses on the 2023 leadership crisis at OpenAI. The movie, which stars Andrew Garfield as OpenAI CEO Sam Altman, is reportedly near completion and is now being offered to other studios for distribution. Amazon stated that it continues to respect Guadagnino and believes the project would be better served by another distributor. The film is described as a comedic drama centered on the events surrounding Altman’s brief removal from OpenAI in 2023 and his return just four days later after a power struggle involving the company’s board, employees, and major partners. The cast also includes portrayals of several prominent technology figures, including Elon Musk, Mira Murati, and Ilya Sutskever. Reports from industry sources suggest that the final version of the film became significantly darker in tone than originally planned, with Altman and Musk depicted in an unsympathetic manner.

item.title: JD Vance's New Book 'Communion' Receives Overwhelmingly Negative Reviews
item.description: Vice President JD Vance released his new book titled 'Communion: Finding My Way Back to Faith' this week, which chronicles his religious journey from atheism back to Christianity and his conversion to Catholicism. Professional critics, including The Wall Street Journal, have criticized the book for 'egregious sloppiness' in its arguments and writing. Amateur reviewers on platforms like Barnes & Noble have been even harsher, with the majority of reviews giving it one star. Many verified purchasers described the book as poorly written, questioning Vance's authenticity on faith matters given his political actions and statements. One reviewer called it 'trash written by the most vile and despised humans on this earth,' while another noted the disconnect between Vance's claims about religion and his everyday behavior. Sites like Amazon and Goodreads reportedly limited or suspended reviews due to the volume of negative feedback. The book has sparked discussions about Vance's public image, hi

item.title: Paula Abdul’s Teenage Years: Academic Success, Dance Training, and the Start of Her Career
item.description:
The article looks back at the teenage years of Paula Abdul, highlighting the determination and talent that helped launch her future career in entertainment. Born in 1962 and raised in California’s San Fernando Valley, Abdul attended Van Nuys High School, where she excelled both academically and socially. She was an honor student and served as class president, demonstrating leadership qualities long before becoming a well-known performer.
Dance played a central role in her life from an early age. Inspired by Gene Kelly’s performances, she dedicated herself to years of training. However, Abdul faced significant obstacles. Born prematurely, she experienced health complications and was diagnosed with hip dysplasia. Medical experts often suggested that her physical condition would make a professional dance career difficult. In addition, she eventually realized that her height limited her opportunities in classical ballet and shifted her focus to jazz, tap, and contemporary dance styles.
W

item.title: Sen. Cornyn relays concerns about Pentagon funding as Hegseth seeks support for $350B military spending plan
item.description: According to a report by Raw Story, Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth recently raised concerns in meetings with Senate Republicans about the Pentagon’s available funding for weapons procurement, warning that resources are becoming insufficient to meet national defense needs. His remarks were later relayed to The New York Times by Sen. John Cornyn (R-TX), who paraphrased Hegseth as saying the Pentagon is running short on funding needed to acquire weapons, missiles, and other defense systems. Cornyn, who recently lost his re-election bid after President Donald Trump endorsed a challenger in the Republican primary, has been described in the report as part of a group of GOP lawmakers who have been sidelined politically but may still influence legislative debates. The report also notes that Hegseth is seeking to build support for a proposed $350 billion special budget bill aimed at increasing military spending. However, the initiative has reportedly encountered resistance from both Republican

item.title: Washington Residents Criticize Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool Restoration After Algae Problems Emerge
item.description:
Residents of Washington, D.C. expressed strong criticism of the recently completed $14 million restoration of the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool, a project promoted by President Donald Trump as part of a broader effort to beautify the nation’s capital. According to reports highlighted by CNN, many visitors were disappointed with the pool’s appearance shortly after the renovation was completed. Observers noted that the water had turned green due to algae growth and that much of the blue paint applied to the pool’s bottom appeared to be peeling away or no longer visible.
The project has also attracted controversy because the contract was reportedly awarded through a no-bid process to a contractor with ties to the president, raising concerns among critics about transparency and procurement practices. Prior to completion, experts had warned that painting the bottom of the pool blue could contribute to maintenance issues and would not prevent algae growth.
Federal workers have attempted

item.title: Tim Cook Warns Rising Memory Chip Costs Will Likely Increase iPhone 18 Prices
item.description: Apple CEO Tim Cook has publicly warned that upcoming Apple products, particularly the expected iPhone 18 lineup, may become more expensive due to a severe and ongoing increase in memory chip costs. Speaking to The Wall Street Journal, Cook explained that Apple has been absorbing higher component expenses throughout 2026 in an effort to protect consumers from price increases. However, he stated that the situation has become unsustainable and that passing some of those costs on to customers is now unavoidable. Although Cook did not specify exact products or pricing changes, the timing of his comments suggests that the iPhone 18 could be affected when it launches later this year. Industry estimates cited in the report indicate that memory and storage components for the iPhone 18 Pro could cost Apple approximately $150 more per device than similar components used in the iPhone 17 Pro. The shortage is reportedly being driven in part by growing demand from artificial intelligence companies,

item.title: Union Leader Criticizes Emergency Response Procedures After Fourth Death at USPS Palmetto Distribution Center
item.description:
A union leader is raising concerns about safety and emergency response procedures at the U.S. Postal Service's Palmetto Regional Distribution Center in Georgia following the death of a fourth employee since the facility opened in 2024. Demarcus Little Sr., 45, died after suffering a medical emergency at the approximately one-million-square-foot facility. According to reports cited in the article, CPR was initiated 18 minutes after a 911 call was made. USPS has not publicly disclosed the cause of death, citing employee privacy.
Jonathan Smith, president of the American Postal Workers Union, criticized the facility for lacking emergency phones despite repeated requests for their installation. He argued that the building's size and lack of reliable cell phone service can significantly delay emergency responses. Smith said he first requested emergency phones in late 2025 and was told they would be installed in early 2026, but the project had not been completed by the time of Little's deat

item.title: Columnist Argues Trump's Misreading of Foreign Nationalism Is Undermining His Second-Term Agenda
item.description:
A Politico analysis by Alexander Burns, highlighted by Raw Story, argues that President Donald Trump's second administration is being weakened by a fundamental misunderstanding of nationalism outside the United States. According to the analysis, Trump built his political identity around American sovereignty and national pride but has failed to recognize that leaders and voters in other countries are motivated by similar patriotic instincts. Burns contends that this miscalculation has complicated Trump's foreign policy objectives, strained relationships with international allies on the political right, and created political challenges for Republicans at home.
The article cites several examples. Alberta Premier Danielle Smith, despite her ideological alignment with many conservative American figures, rejected the idea of foreign involvement in Canadian politics, emphasizing national sovereignty. Similarly, Canadian resistance to Trump's tariff threats and rhetoric is described as contri

item.title: MSNBC Analyst John Heilemann Argues Trump's Recent Nicknames Reflect Cognitive Decline
item.description: A Raw Story article reports on comments made by MSNBC political analyst John Heilemann during an appearance on the program 'Deadline: White House.' During a discussion with host Nicolle Wallace, Heilemann criticized President Donald Trump’s leadership and argued that several recent actions and public remarks demonstrate declining competence. Wallace contended that Trump is disconnected from the concerns of ordinary Americans and lacks empathy for their economic and personal challenges. Heilemann agreed with that assessment and pointed to what he described as administrative failures, including the condition of the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool, which he said symbolized broader governmental mismanagement. He also referenced tensions involving Iran as another example of what he characterized as poor decision-making and incompetence within the administration. Heilemann’s central argument focused on Trump’s use of political nicknames. He compared well-known labels from previous election

item.title: Opinion Column Argues Trump's Support for Bill Pulte Reflects Concerns About Future Congressional Investigations
item.description: This opinion article by Jason Miciak examines President Donald Trump's decision to keep Bill Pulte as acting Director of National Intelligence (DNI) despite opposition from many Republican senators and after withdrawing Pulte's formal nomination. The author argues that the move is highly unusual because Trump bypassed a planned Senate confirmation process for another nominee, Jay Clayton, and instead retained a loyal ally in a key intelligence position. According to the columnist, Pulte's primary qualification is his demonstrated loyalty to Trump, particularly through actions taken while serving in government roles. The article speculates that Trump may be preparing for potential political challenges following the 2026 midterm elections, especially the possibility of Democrats gaining control of Congress and launching investigations into his administration. Miciak suggests that having a trusted loyalist overseeing national intelligence could provide Trump with a mechanism to support cl

item.title: A Retrospective of Frances Pellegrini’s Fashion Photography from the 1950s
item.description: This article from Vintage Everyday highlights the work of American fashion photographer Frances Pellegrini, whose elegant and distinctive images helped define fashion photography during the 1940s and 1950s. Known for her refined compositions, soft lighting, and natural poses, Pellegrini offered an alternative to the more dramatic and highly stylized fashion imagery that was common during the era. Her photographs captured both the beauty of the clothing and the individuality of the models, giving her work a timeless and artistic quality. The feature presents an extensive gallery of photographs originally published in Harper’s Bazaar between 1953 and 1958. The images showcase a wide range of postwar fashion trends, including formal gowns, tailored suits, coats, lingerie, evening wear, accessories, footwear, and leisure clothing. Models featured in the collection include Evelyn Tripp, Tippi Hedren, Elsa Martinelli, Carmen Dell'Orefice, Mary Jane Russell, Jessica Ford, Isabella Albonico, L

item.title: Elon Musk’s Departure from South Africa Was Driven by Career Ambitions and Political Uncertainty, Says His Father
item.description:
